abstract: This article explores the missional potential of the biblical books of Ezra and Nehemiah, viewed as a single, two-volume literary unit. We argue that the written Word of God acts as a transformative force within society, primarily through the mediation and witness of the church. This dynamic suggests that spiritual renewal is a necessary prerequisite for lasting social reconstruction. To build a robust foundation for this missiological reflection, we examine the text through historical, canonical, and exegetical lenses, specifically considering the role of sacred scripture in the transition from an oral to a literate culture. We conclude by offering missional guidelines intended to support the development of a contemporary and socially relevant theology of mission.
